_doc: T510: OpenHands reads `.openhands/hooks.json` (snake_case event keys; per-event array of { matcher, hooks:[{command,timeout}] }; `command` is a shell command or script path; stdin JSON payload; exit 2 or {"decision":"deny"} blocks). Each native event routes to a thin canonical entrypoint `python .openhands/hooks/g-hk-on-<event>.py` which calls `g_hk_core.dispatch("<event>")`. The shared core `.openhands/hooks/g_hk_core.py` is byte-identical across every platform overlay and runs the per-event CONCERN_CHAIN (concern hooks self-gate on payload). Native->canonical map (g_hk_core.PLATFORM_EVENT_MAP['openhands']): SessionStart->session-start, SessionEnd->session-end, UserPromptSubmit->user-prompt-submit, PreToolUse->tool-start, PostToolUse->tool-end, Stop->stop. timeout is in seconds. Schema verified against https://docs.openhands.dev/openhands/usage/customization/hooks .
